strategy: fp16 + channels_last cuDNN convs (fp32-accumulate) — half-precision intermediate halves the dominant HBM term & 2x tenso

sources.content:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

# --- B200 cuDNN / tensor-core tuning (set once at import) ---------------------
# Loose tolerance (atol ~0.06-0.078, rtol 1e-5) leaves ample room for 10-bit
# mantissa tensor cores.  fp16 convs on cuDNN accumulate in fp32, so the only
# precision loss is input rounding — well inside budget (precedent: the sibling
# VAE-resblock problem passes fp16 at atol 3e-3, ~20x tighter than here).
torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
# Autotune the conv algorithm per (fixed) shape — picks Winograd/implicit-GEMM.
torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True

_HALF = torch.float16
_CL = torch.channels_last


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    conv_in_weight: torch.Tensor,
    conv_in_bias: torch.Tensor,
    conv_out_weight: torch.Tensor,
    conv_out_bias: torch.Tensor,
):
    """
    Fused-precision conv2d residual block (3->32->3, 3x3, pad=1) + residual.

    Levers for these 16 shapes:
      * fp16 storage: the (B,32,H,W) intermediate is 10.7x larger than the I/O
        and is the dominant HBM traffic term.  Holding it in half-precision
        halves that traffic on every memory-bound shape (#0,#1,#3,#6,#7,#11,
        #14,#15) and doubles tensor-core throughput vs TF32.
      * channels_last (NHWC): cuDNN's tensor-core conv path; the 32-channel
        second conv in particular loves NHWC.
      * cudnn.benchmark: per-shape algorithm autotune (Winograd F(2x2,3x3)
        cuts the 3x3 arithmetic 2.25x for free where applicable).
      * residual added back in fp32 against the ORIGINAL input, so the skip
        path is exact and only the (small) conv correction carries fp16 error.
    """
    # Cast inputs + weights to fp16 / NHWC.  Weight casts are tiny (<7 KB) and
    # cheap; we deliberately do NOT cache them (allocator can reuse addresses,
    # which turns data_ptr caching into a correctness hazard).
    xh = x.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    w1 = conv_in_weight.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    w2 = conv_out_weight.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    b1 = conv_in_bias.to(_HALF)
    b2 = conv_out_bias.to(_HALF)

    out = F.conv2d(xh, w1, b1, padding=1)   # (B,32,H,W) fp16 NHWC
    out = F.conv2d(out, w2, b2, padding=1)  # (B, 3,H,W) fp16 NHWC

    # Residual in fp32 against the original fp32 input; output dtype fp32.
    # This allocates a fresh tensor (no static-buffer aliasing).
    return out.to(torch.float32) + x

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

# --- B200 cuDNN / tensor-core tuning (set once at import) ---------------------
# Loose tolerance (atol ~0.06-0.078, rtol 1e-5) leaves ample room for 10-bit
# mantissa tensor cores.  fp16 convs on cuDNN accumulate in fp32, so the only
# precision loss is input rounding — well inside budget (precedent: the sibling
# VAE-resblock problem passes fp16 at atol 3e-3, ~20x tighter than here).
torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
# Autotune the conv algorithm per (fixed) shape — picks Winograd/implicit-GEMM.
torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True

_HALF = torch.float16
_CL = torch.channels_last


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    conv_in_weight: torch.Tensor,
    conv_in_bias: torch.Tensor,
    conv_out_weight: torch.Tensor,
    conv_out_bias: torch.Tensor,
):
    """
    Fused-precision conv2d residual block (3->32->3, 3x3, pad=1) + residual.

    Levers for these 16 shapes:
      * fp16 storage: the (B,32,H,W) intermediate is 10.7x larger than the I/O
        and is the dominant HBM traffic term.  Holding it in half-precision
        halves that traffic on every memory-bound shape (#0,#1,#3,#6,#7,#11,
        #14,#15) and doubles tensor-core throughput vs TF32.
      * channels_last (NHWC): cuDNN's tensor-core conv path; the 32-channel
        second conv in particular loves NHWC.
      * cudnn.benchmark: per-shape algorithm autotune (Winograd F(2x2,3x3)
        cuts the 3x3 arithmetic 2.25x for free where applicable).
      * residual added back in fp32 against the ORIGINAL input, so the skip
        path is exact and only the (small) conv correction carries fp16 error.
    """
    # Cast inputs + weights to fp16 / NHWC.  Weight casts are tiny (<7 KB) and
    # cheap; we deliberately do NOT cache them (allocator can reuse addresses,
    # which turns data_ptr caching into a correctness hazard).
    xh = x.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    w1 = conv_in_weight.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    w2 = conv_out_weight.to(dtype=_HALF, memory_format=_CL)
    b1 = conv_in_bias.to(_HALF)
    b2 = conv_out_bias.to(_HALF)

    out = F.conv2d(xh, w1, b1, padding=1)   # (B,32,H,W) fp16 NHWC
    out = F.conv2d(out, w2, b2, padding=1)  # (B, 3,H,W) fp16 NHWC

    # Residual in fp32 against the original fp32 input; output dtype fp32.
    # This allocates a fresh tensor (no static-buffer aliasing).
    return out.to(torch.float32) + x
